post.title: Who agrees Jesus is God?
post.body: Mar 11  • 🍞 Bible SnacksWho agrees Jesus is God?Yes Jesus And God Are OneNo Jesus is not the same as God🤷🏾‍♂️62 votes

comment.author: Nasaiah McEwan
comment.text: Nasaiah McEwan • Mar 12Hey guys question what is considered as gossip is it like talking bad about someone when their not around or is it telling lies on someone when their not around or just talking about someone in general when they’re not around?

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 11 (edited)Genesis 1:26 proves God is triune and John 1:1-3

thread.author: Christiano Vibes
thread.text: Christiano Vibes🔥 • Mar 11 (edited)Im pretty confused about it myself I've always thought jesus is the son of god so while he has a similar role he's like different yet the same. Its also tricky to ask some as they get a lil intense. Personally I feel like I dont know & wouldnt even know without picking a denomination to reference how I'd know for certain what jesus's "god status" is.Its like I dont have to understand his job to understand him & have devotion.Similarly I dont feel pressure to d

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 11@Christiano Vibes In Genesis 1:26, God said let US make man in our image. So he had to be talking about more than one person. He was talking about Him, the Son and Holy Spirit.John 1:1In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.John 1:14And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we beheld His glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth.”Jesus was the Word that became flesh while still being God al

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 11There’s also more passages as well that shows that Jesus is God too but these are the ones that come to mind first that I refer to has actually helped me understand the Holy Trinity not to a point where I fully get it but I know that scripture tells us that there’s one God but three distinct persons.

thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 12@Nasaiah McEwan you answered your question with the two questions you asked.

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 12@Nasaiah McEwan you can talk about someone when they’re not around long as it’s not in a slanderous or disrespectful way.Ex:Person 1: what you think about John?Person 2: I love john but he really got to do better with how he speak to womenThat’s a respectful way of speaking of someone who needs to fix a behavior or theirs.

post.author: Cristain Harris
post.title: Newbie FOMO
post.body: Mar 11  • 📰 Faith and CultureNewbie FOMOHi, Hope Fam! I'm new to the group. I love reading and studying the Bible. All of the stories, parables, and events all play key roles in our society and mindset today just as they did 2000 years ago. I often read Scripture and feel like I am missing things. I sometimes read things literally, and in the Bible things are almost never literal lol. Anyone else ever feel like their missing something when it comes to studying?

generation.reply: The way I see it, acknowledging something is a process, like phases. You're at a point of wanting to know and ready to acknowledge the truth. You've already entered the circle pretty deep, now is the time to really get into the core. I know it's going to be worth it in the end.

post.author: Ben Matthew
post.title: entrepreneurship is a faith walk
post.body: Feb 19  • 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 The Hope Famentrepreneurship is a faith walkOne thing I’ve been learning on this business journey is that faith isn’t just something we talk about on Sundays… it shows up in how we handle uncertainty, delays, and even success.There are moments when things don’t move as fast as we want, doors don’t open when we expect, or plans don’t work out the way we imagined, And in those moments, trust becomes real.For me, entrepreneurship has been one of the biggest faith-walks God has ever taken me on. It’s stretched me, humbled me, and grown me in ... See more

comment.author: Ben Matthew
comment.text: @Daniel Carter hello Daniel so sorry I'm just getting to reply your message. i don't think i understand what you mean by " Yes, it is " are you saying business has challenge your faith in any way, what business are you into btw?

thread.author: Amber Mitchell
thread.text: Amber Mitchell • Feb 19This is so real. Entrepreneurship has absolutely been a faith walk for me. I have been in the beauty industry for 26 years and a barbershop owner for 20 of those years, and it has definitely had its ebbs and flows. I even tried going back to a 9 to 5 a couple of times thinking that was the safer move, but I felt completely out of alignment. I realized this is part of my calling.It has not been unicorns and roses, especially raising two boys as a single mom when I first ste

thread.author: Ben Matthew
thread.text: Ben Matthew • Feb 20@Amber Mitchell Wow… your story is powerful. The part about stepping out with little money, raising your boys, and still trusting God that takes real courage and faith. And now seeing your son walk in entrepreneurship too, that’s legacy for real. You didn’t just build a business, you built an example.I also love what you said about feeling “out of alignment” going back to a 9–5. That’s usually such a clear sign when we’re walking in something we’re actually called to.One thin

thread.author: Amber Mitchell
thread.text: Amber Mitchell • Feb 20@Ben Matthew Thank you, that truly means a lot. I’m grateful my story resonated with you.And yes, I have definitely thought about building something beyond the chair. For a while now, God has put it on my heart to open a Barber and Beauty School in my area. post.title: Who agrees Jesus is God?
post.body: Mar 11  • 🍞 Bible SnacksWho agrees Jesus is God?Yes Jesus And God Are OneNo Jesus is not the same as God🤷🏾‍♂️62 votes

comment.author: Nasaiah McEwan
comment.text: Nasaiah McEwan • Mar 13Do y’all believe that people may over-spiritualize certain things?

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 11 (edited)Genesis 1:26 proves God is triune and John 1:1-3

thread.author: Christiano Vibes
thread.text: Christiano Vibes🔥 • Mar 11 (edited)Im pretty confused about it myself I've always thought jesus is the son of god so while he has a similar role he's like different yet the same. Its also tricky to ask some as they get a lil intense. Personally I feel like I dont know & wouldnt even know without picking a denomination to reference how I'd know for certain what jesus's "god status" is.Its like I dont have to understand his job to understand him & have devotion.Similarly I dont feel pressure to d

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 11@Christiano Vibes In Genesis 1:26, God said let US make man in our image. So he had to be talking about more than one person. He was talking about Him, the Son and Holy Spirit.John 1:1In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.John 1:14And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we beheld His glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth.”Jesus was the Word that became flesh while still being God al

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 11There’s also more passages as well that shows that Jesus is God too but these are the ones that come to mind first that I refer to has actually helped me understand the Holy Trinity not to a point where I fully get it but I know that scripture tells us that there’s one God but three distinct persons.

thread.author: DeJuan Dennard
thread.text: DeJuan Dennard • Mar 12@Nasaiah McEwan you can talk about someone when they’re not around long as it’s not in a slanderous or disrespectful way.Ex:Person 1: what you think about John?Person 2: I love john but he really got to do better with how he speak to womenThat’s a respectful way of speaking of someone who needs to fix a behavior or theirs.

thread.author: Kris Jim Murdock
thread.text: Kris Jim Murdock • Mar 17Jesus is all of God there ever has been or will be, and He manifested Himself in flesh as the Only-Begotten Son so He would have a body and blood to sacrifice for our sins

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: Ugly or beautiful, the truth will always set you free.

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: The way I see it, acknowledging something is a process, like phases. You're at a point of wanting to know and ready to acknowledge the truth. You've already entered the circle pretty deep, now is the time to really get into the core. I know it's going to be worth it in the end.

Be encouraged and I hope you all have a great week! Love you all my brothers and sisters in Christ! 💯
